violations.text: The non-food contact surfaces of the prep units and walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026
The floor tiles in the kitchen area are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/03/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 200 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Call for service was placed during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers inside the prep unit. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls were removed during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the low boy freezer in the kitchen. Refrigerator door gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the prep units and walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2025
The floor tiles in the kitchen area are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/03/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment (Ice Machine) were observed with an accumulation of unidentified black residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice Machine shall be cleaned and routinely cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023

Cold hold unit in Kitchen was observed held at (52) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were observed between 40-41 degrees. Items in unit were relocated to walk-in. Service repair was called at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023
The condensate from bar taps at bar unit is draining into a bucket. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023
